title: IGCSE Computer Science Boolean Logic
subject: Computer Science
materials: Printed worksheet or shared digital copy.
summary: Targeted practice for logic gates, Boolean expressions, and truth tables.
learning_objectives: Complete truth-table outputs for common logic gates.
Translate simple real-world rules into Boolean expressions.
curriculum_references: Cambridge IGCSE Computer Science 0478: logic gates
Cambridge IGCSE Computer Science 0478: truth tables

standards: Cambridge IGCSE Computer Science 0478: logic gates
items.prompt: Complete the output for A AND B when A = 1 and B = 0.
items.explanation: An AND gate only outputs 1 when both inputs are 1.

standards: Cambridge IGCSE Computer Science 0478: logic gates
Cambridge IGCSE Computer Science 0478: truth tables
items.prompt: Write the Boolean expression for a NOT gate applied to input X.
items.explanation: A NOT gate reverses a single input.
rubric: 1 mark for applying it to X.

standards: Cambridge IGCSE Computer Science 0478: Boolean expressions
items.prompt: A system sounds an alarm when motion is detected OR the door is opened. Write this as a Boolean expression using M and D.
items.explanation: The alarm is active when either condition is true.
rubric: 1 mark for connecting the conditions with OR.
